info.name: Share Audit Dashboard
info.summary: Visual audit dashboard for all shares across your instance
info.description:
See and audit every share on your Nextcloud — in the browser, not the command line.
Share Audit Dashboard gives administrators a single, visual overview of every share on the instance (user, group, public link, email, federated, Talk), flags the risky ones, and lets you fix them in bulk. Regular users get a personal view to audit and clean up the files they share.
**For administrators (Settings → Administration → Share Audit):** - Dashboard: totals per share type, a 12‑month creation trend, an internal‑vs‑external exposure donut, top sharers, and attention banners for insecure links and orphaned shares. - All shares: a filterable, sortable, server‑side paginated table with per‑column header filters and human‑readable permissions. Export the filtered view to CSV. - Security alerts: public links with no password, no expiration, or a sensitive file type — fixable individually or in bulk (add a generated password, set an expiration, revoke). Rules configurable. - Orphan shares: shares still owned by disabled or deleted accounts, with bulk revoke. - Exposure map: internal / external / public reach, a 0‑100 exposure score and the top public sharers. - Access lookup: search a user, group or email and see every file they can reach, with revoke‑all.
**For every user (Settings → Personal → My shares audit):** - Review the files you share and fix your own risky public links (add password / set expiration / revoke), plus a dashboard widget highlighting the links that need your attention.
Unlike the CLI‑only sharelisting app, everything is available in the browser.
info.author: Ricardo Ferreira
